trails.name: Skyline Trail
trails.description: An iconic loop starting from Paradise, offering subalpine meadows, marmot sightings, and up-close views of the Nisqually Glacier.

trails.name: Trail of Ten Falls
trails.description: A world-class canyon trail that passes ten distinct waterfalls, several of which you can walk behind.

trails.name: Colchuck Lake
trails.description: A steep climb to a stunning turquoise alpine lake sitting at the base of Dragontail and Colchuck Peaks.

trails.name: Misery Ridge Loop
trails.description: A high-desert trek featuring unique basalt cliffs, views of the Monkey Face pillar, and the Crooked River.

trails.name: Mount Storm King
trails.description: A steep, unmaintained ascent involving rope assists that rewards hikers with a panoramic view of Lake Crescent.

trails.name: Multnomah Falls & Wahkeena Loop
trails.description: Combines the most famous waterfall in Oregon with several smaller falls and misty canyon views.

trails.name: Hall of Mosses
trails.description: An easy walk through a lush, temperate rainforest draped in club moss and ancient ferns.

trails.name: Tamolitch Blue Pool
trails.description: A scenic hike along the McKenzie River to a surreal, vibrant blue pool where the river emerges from an underground lava tube.

trails.name: Heather-Maple Pass Loop
trails.description: Widely considered one of the best 'bang-for-your-buck' hikes for jagged mountain peaks and fall larch colors.

trails.name: Mirror Lake
trails.description: A popular, family-friendly trail leading to a lake that provides a perfect reflection of Mount Hood.

trails.name: Rattlesnake Ledge
trails.description: A well-maintained, steady climb to a rocky outcrop overlooking Rattlesnake Lake and the Snoqualmie Valley.

trails.name: Cape Flattery Trail
trails.description: A boardwalk trail leading to the northwesternmost point of the contiguous US, featuring dramatic sea sea stacks.

trails.name: South Sister
trails.description: A grueling non-technical climb to the summit of Oregon's third-tallest peak, offering views of the entire Cascade range.

trails.name: Twin Falls
trails.description: A pleasant, forested trail along the South Fork Snoqualmie River leading to three different waterfall viewpoints.

trails.name: God's Thumb
trails.description: A coastal hike through a 'ghost forest' to a basalt headland that looks like a giant thumb overlooking the Pacific.
